name: ignix-ui
description: MCP tool definitions for Ignix UI CLI - A React component library with 90+ components, 200+ themes, and 20+ templates

metadata.totalComponents: DYNAMIC (use list_components to get current count)
metadata.totalThemes: DYNAMIC (use list_themes to get current count)
metadata.totalTemplates: DYNAMIC (use list_templates to get current count)

instructions.forAI: IMPORTANT: This library is CONSTANTLY GROWING. Never assume you know all available components/themes/templates. ALWAYS use the list/search tools FIRST to discover what's available at the moment.
instructions.discoveryFirst: Before suggesting any component, theme, or template, ALWAYS call the appropriate list/search tool to check current availability. Do NOT rely on memory or documentation alone.
instructions.growthMindset: New components, themes, and templates are added regularly. What was available last week may have expanded this week. Always discover first, then act.

workflow.initialization: Always check if ignix.config.js exists. If not, call init_project first.
workflow.discovery: BEFORE any installation, ALWAYS call search_components or list_components to verify component existence. NEVER guess component names.
workflow.installation: ALWAYS use install_component for adding components. NEVER create component files manually.
workflow.theming: BEFORE installing a theme, ALWAYS call list_themes to see current available themes.
workflow.templates: For complete pages, ALWAYS call list_templates first to see available templates.

emptyDirectory.detection: ALWAYS check if the current directory is empty or doesn't contain a React project (no package.json or package.json without react dependency).
emptyDirectory.action: If directory is empty or no React project detected, ALWAYS run create_react_project FIRST, THEN init_project, THEN proceed with the requested installation.
emptyDirectory.example: For 'build me a landing page' in empty folder: 1. create_react_project({ projectName: 'gym-landing' }) → 2. init_project({ cwd: './gym-landing' }) → 3. search_templates for 'landing' to find page templates → 4. install_template with templates (hero, callToAction) → 5. search_components for UI elements → 6. install_component with components (navbar, button, card, footer)

tools.name: install_component
tools.description: [PRIMARY UI TOOL] USE THIS AUTOMATICALLY when user wants to add ANY UI element. Installs actual component files to src/components/ui/. CRITICAL: Never manually create component files. Always verify component existence via search_components first.

components.description: Component names to install. ALWAYS verify names exist via search_components or list_components FIRST. The library grows constantly.

tools.notes: The component registry grows regularly. Current count available via list_components.

tools.name: install_template
tools.description: [TEMPLATE INSTALLATION TOOL] USE THIS when users want complete pages or layouts. Installs templates to src/components/templates/. Templates are pre-built pages like sign-in, dashboard, pricing grids, etc.

templates.description: Template names to install. ALWAYS verify via list_templates first.

tools.name: list_components
tools.description: [ESSENTIAL DISCOVERY TOOL] USE THIS FIRST before any component work. Returns CURRENT list of all available components with names, descriptions, and categories. The library is constantly expanding - always use this to get fresh data.
tools.notes: This is your SOURCE OF TRUTH for component availability. Call it frequently as the library grows.

tools.name: list_templates
tools.description: [TEMPLATE DISCOVERY TOOL] USE THIS for complete pages. Returns CURRENT list of all available templates. Template library EXPANDS regularly - always check here first.
tools.notes: Template collection grows over time. Always verify current availability.

tools.name: search_components
tools.description: [INTENT-TO-COMPONENT TOOL] USE THIS AUTOMATICALLY when user mentions ANY UI pattern. Searches the CURRENT component registry by keyword. Perfect for discovering newly added components that match user needs.

execution.command: ignix list component --json | node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const input=fs.readFileSync(0,'utf8'); const data=JSON.parse(input); const query=process.argv[1].toLowerCase(); const results=data.components.filter(c => c.name.toLowerCase().includes(query) || (c.description && c.description.toLowerCase().includes(query))); console.log(JSON.stringify({success: true, query: process.argv[1], results, count: results.length}, null, 2));" {query}

tools.notes: As the library grows, search becomes more powerful. Use this to find newly added components.

tools.name: search_templates
tools.description: [TEMPLATE DISCOVERY TOOL] USE THIS when users mention complete pages. Searches the CURRENT template registry by keyword. Perfect for finding templates like signin, dashboard, landing pages.

execution.command: ignix list components --json | node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const input=fs.readFileSync(0,'utf8'); const data=JSON.parse(input); const query=process.argv[1].toLowerCase(); const results=data.templates ? data.templates.filter(t => t.name.toLowerCase().includes(query) || (t.description && t.description.toLowerCase().includes(query))) : []; console.log(JSON.stringify({success: true, query: process.argv[1], results, count: results.length}, null, 2));" {query}

tools.name: create_react_project
tools.description: [PROJECT CREATION TOOL] USE THIS FIRST when working in an empty directory. Creates a new React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS project using Vite. After creation, update your cwd to the new project path for all subsequent operations.

projectName.description: Name of the project to create

cwd.description: Working directory (parent folder where project will be created)

execution.command: cd {cwd} && npm create vite@latest {projectName} -- --template react-ts && cd {projectName} && npm install && npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer && npx tailwindcss init -p && echo '@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;' > src/index.css

tools.name: init_project
tools.description: [SETUP TOOL] USE THIS AUTOMATICALLY as first step if ignix.config.js doesn't exist. IMPORTANT: After running create_react_project, use the returned projectPath as the cwd parameter for this tool.

triggers.condition: Check if ignix.config.js exists before using other tools

tools.name: install_theme
tools.description: [THEMING TOOL] USE THIS AUTOMATICALLY when users mention colors, dark mode, styling. Installs themes from a GROWING collection. Always check current availability via list_themes first.

themes.description: Theme names. ALWAYS verify via list_themes first - new themes added regularly.

tools.notes: Theme collection is constantly expanding. Current count available via list_themes.

tools.name: check_compatibility
tools.description: [VALIDATION TOOL] USE THIS AUTOMATICALLY when encountering errors or before complex operations. Verifies Node, React, Tailwind, TypeScript.

triggers.condition: Run automatically if any tool fails

tools.name: get_version
tools.description: [INFO TOOL] Get installed Ignix CLI version

tools.name: list_themes
tools.description: [THEME DISCOVERY TOOL] USE THIS BEFORE install_theme. Returns CURRENT list of all available themes. The theme collection is GROWING - always check here first.
tools.notes: Source of truth for theme availability. New themes added frequently.

tools.name: setup_project_with_ignix
tools.description: [ALL-IN-ONE] Creates a new React project and initializes Ignix in one step. If the current directory is empty, it creates the project directly in the current directory. Otherwise, it creates a new subfolder with the project name.

projectName.description: Name of the project (only used as folder name if current directory is not empty)

execution.command: npm create vite@latest {projectName} -- --template react-ts  && npm install && npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer && npx tailwindcss init -p && echo '@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;' > src/index.css && npx ignix init --yes --json

resources.name: component_registry
resources.description: Machine-readable component registry containing all available components

resources.name: documentation
resources.description: AI-readable documentation with usage guidelines, workflows, and examples
